description:
You are the lead epidemiologist investigating a cryptosporidiosis outbreak of unknown source in a city. A survey of 200 residents across 5 neighborhoods is available at C:\Users\Docker\Documents\outbreak_survey.csv.

The data requires cleaning before analysis. The Sex column contains mixed formats (M/Male/male/F/Female/female) — standardize to Male and Female. The WaterSource column has inconsistent entries (Tap/tap/Municipal/municipal/Bottled/Well) — standardize into two categories: Municipal and Other. The Ill column has mixed formats (Yes/Y/1/No/N/0) — standardize to Yes and No. Create a binary variable MunicipalWater (Yes if cleaned WaterSource equals Municipal, else No) and a binary variable HighConsumption (Yes if DailyGlasses is 5 or more, else No).

Using Epi Info Classic Analysis, investigate the outbreak: compute the overall attack rate, determine which neighborhood has the highest attack rate, evaluate each exposure (MunicipalWater, UsesFilter, SwimmingPool, DaycareContact, HighConsumption) as a risk factor for illness using 2x2 tables, run multivariable logistic regression with Ill as outcome and all 5 exposures as predictors to identify any confounder (an exposure significant in crude analysis but not after adjustment), and generate an epidemic curve of OnsetDate for ill cases.

Save findings to C:\Users\Docker\Documents\outbreak_report.txt containing: overall attack rate, highest-rate neighborhood and its rate, the primary risk factor with its crude risk ratio, adjusted odds ratio from logistic regression, any identified confounder, and whether DailyGlasses shows a dose-response relationship.
